LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. THE COURT FOCUSES ON THE ACTIONS OF THE INSURED RATHER THAN THE FORM OF THE PROCEEDINGS. ONCE DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THE CONCURRENT CONSTRUCTION DEFECT ACTION, IT OWED A DUTY TO DEFEND AND INDEMNIFY BOTH RELATED ACTIONS. DEFENDANT ALSO OWED A DUTY TO APPOINT INDEPENDENT COUNSEL. (ORDER RE: STATE FARMS OBJECTIONS FILED IN OPEN COURT AND COUNSEL RECEIVE COPY AT HEARING.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: BOB BALIAN, CRS #5220

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF VERA POON MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED (AMENDED AS TO #6) ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART. MOTION GRANTED AS TO #1, DEFENDANT HAS A DUTY TO INDEMNIFY PLAINTIFF UNDER THE POLICIES FOR THE ARBITRATION AWARD. #3, DEFENDANT HAS A DUTY TO INDEMNIFY PLAINTIFF FOR THE FEES/COSTS INCURRED IN THE ARBITRATION AWARD. #7, THE 10TH DEFENSE FAILS BASED ON WAIVER AND ESTOPPEL. #8, THE 9TH DEFENSE FAILS BASED ON WAIVER AND ESTOPPEL. AND 	, THE 7TH DEFENSE FAILS BASED ON WAIVER AND ESTOPPEL. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED AS TO: #6, THE 16TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE, AND, ALSO REMAINING ISSUES UNDER CCP SECTION 437C(F)(1). (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: BOB BALIAN, CRS #5220

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Feb-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF VERA POON MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Michael A. Mathews, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 693-0110 appearing for Plaintiff and Moving party; Clarke B. Holland, Esq. & Jenny J. Chu, Esq. of LHB Pacific Law Partners LLP (510) 841-7777 appearing for Defendant State Farm and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended (amended as to #6) its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary adjudication is granted in part. Motion granted as to #1, defendant has a duty to indemnify plaintiff under the policies for the arbitration award. #3, defendant has a duty to indemnify plaintiff for the fees/costs incurred in the arbitration award. #7, the 10th defense fails based on waiver and estoppel. #8, the 9th defense fails based on waiver and estoppel. And , the 7th defense fails based on waiver and estoppel. Summary adjudication denied as to: #6, the 16th affirmative defense, and, also remaining issues under CCP section 437c(f)(1). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Bob Balian, CRS #5220

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Michael A. Mathews, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 693-0110 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Clarke B. Holland, Esq. & Jenny J. Chu, Esq. of LHB Pacific Law Partners LLP (510) 841-7777 appearing for Defendant State Farm and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication denied, failure to shift burden of proof. The court focuses on the actions of the insured rather than the form of the proceedings. Once defendant learned of the concurrent construction defect action, it owed a duty to defend and indemnify both related actions. Defendant also owed a duty to appoint independent counsel. (Order re: state Farms objections filed in Open Court and counsel receive copy at hearing.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Bob Balian, CRS #5220
